context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Green Canyon Block 743

actions_taken: All operations suspended on rig floor to investigate
Frank's casing supervisor verified elevator integrity via man-rider
Casing double safely lowered and set in casing spider
Safety stand-down initiated on drill floor
Different set of casing elevators utilized to complete casing run
West Vela team and BP held Safety Stand Down meeting
event.summary: On January 15, 2021, at 15:26 hours during drilling operations on the Seadrill West Vela in 6,534 feet of water, an 8-pound leveling cone and a 104-pound bell guide insert fell approximately 90 feet from Frank's International spider type casing elevators to the rig floor. The incident occurred when the casing became wedged between the VersaFlo tool and the bell guide due to slight misalignment. The elevators tilted on the Top Drive System bails, causing the leveling cone to shear off and the bell guide insert to dislodge. No injuries were reported. The incident was caused by misalignment between the VersaFlo tool and bell guide, combined with inadequate secondary retention devices and excessive weight on the elevators.
recommendations: Evaluate and adjust camera placement on rig floor during casing runs to improve visibility
Update Seadrill Step-by-Step Procedure to include equipment verification step by Assistant Driller or Driller during rigging up of casing elevators or VersaFlo tool to ensure proper alignment
Frank's International to install more robust secondary retention on leveling cones
Frank's International to explore additional options for secondary retention on bell guide inserts
Update pre-rig up check list to include additional alignment checks during casing run operation
Seadrill to share lessons learned from incident to other vessels in fleet
key_phrases: misalignment between VersaFlo tool and bell guide
26 kips of force applied to align equipment

threats.name: Inadequate secondary retention on elevator components
Misalignment of VersaFlo tool with bell guide during casing run
Excessive force applied to elevators during alignment
Limited visibility of equipment in derrick during casing operations

consequences.name: Dropped objects striking personnel or equipment on rig floor
Damage to casing elevators and associated equipment
Operational delay and loss of productivity

controls.name: Red Zone Management
controls.barrier_role: Exclusion zone to prevent personnel exposure to dropped object hazard
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to limit personnel in hazard area

supporting_text: Seadrill's Red Zone management was in place and active at the time, and the nearest personnel were 15 feet outside the Red Zone.

controls.name: Secondary Retention Device on Leveling Cone
controls.barrier_role: Prevent leveling cone from falling if primary attachment fails
controls.lod_basis: Secondary mechanical retention to prevent dropped objects

supporting_text: The 8-pound leveling cone fell approximately 90 feet to the drill floor after the secondary retention device failed.
Inadequate secondary retention on leveling cones and bell guide inserts.

controls.name: Secondary Retention Device on Bell Guide Insert
controls.barrier_role: Prevent bell guide insert from falling if primary attachment fails
controls.lod_basis: Secondary mechanical retention to prevent dropped objects

supporting_text: The bell guide insert had no secondary retention in place at the time of the incident.
Inadequate secondary retention on leveling cones and bell guide inserts.

controls.name: Frank's Category 2 Inspection of Casing Elevators
controls.barrier_role: Verify structural integrity and proper function of lifting equipment before use
controls.lod_basis: Pre-operation inspection to identify equipment defects

supporting_text: A Frank's category 2 inspection was done on their spider type casing elevators prior to their arrival to the rig on January 7, 2021, along with a completed Frank's jobsite equipment checklist.

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) Pre-Rig Up Inspection
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and verify equipment alignment before casing operations commence
controls.lod_basis: Pre-operation hazard assessment and equipment verification

supporting_text: On January 15, 2021, a Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was completed and signed with no issues or misalignments identified on the equipment during the pre-rig up inspection.

controls.name: Seadrill Step-by-Step Procedure (SSP) for Casing Operations
controls.barrier_role: Provide standardized procedures for safe casing pickup and run operations
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure consistent safe work practices

supporting_text: A Seadrill Step-by-Step Procedure (SSP) titled 'Casing – Prepare, PickUp and Run Casing,' was generated along with a Risk Assessment form that was initialed and signed by all parties involved before the casing run procedure.

controls.name: Thread Inspector Verification
controls.barrier_role: Verify proper thread condition and connection integrity before and after makeup
controls.lod_basis: Quality control inspection to ensure proper equipment condition

supporting_text: Once in position, the thread inspector representative surveyed the casing threads before the lift nubbin was attached to raise the casing.
The thread inspector confirmed that the connection was good and signaled the Driller to lower the elevators over the casing using the TDS.

controls.name: Time Out and Connection Torque Review
controls.barrier_role: Verify proper connection makeup and identify potential issues before proceeding
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to ensure quality of critical connections

supporting_text: After the Frank's casing tong operator made up the connection, a time out was taken to review the digital graph of the connection torque that was displayed on the Frank's computer.

controls.name: Derrick Camera System
controls.barrier_role: Provide visibility to driller of equipment positioning during casing operations
controls.lod_basis: Detection and monitoring control to identify misalignment issues

supporting_text: The Driller had a limited view in the camera in the derrick, so it was not possible to view the VersaFlo tool going into the casing once it entered the bell of the elevators.
The West Vela team will evaluate the camera placement on the rig floor during casing runs and if beneficial the cameras will be adjusted to improve visibility.

controls.name: Frank's Casing Supervisor Post-Incident Verification
controls.barrier_role: Verify structural integrity of elevators after incident before resuming operations
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to assess equipment safety after failure event

supporting_text: A Frank's casing supervisor traveled up to the elevators by way of the man-rider to verify that it was safe to use the elevators to lower the casing double into the well.

controls.name: Top Drive System Creep Mode Operation
controls.barrier_role: Provide controlled lowering of casing elevators to reduce impact forces and misalignment
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to manage forces during critical operation

supporting_text: The Driller proceeded to lower the TDS in 'Creep Mode' with the spider type elevators to latch onto the casing.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
